metadata.title: Framework High-Watermark Crosswalk
metadata.description: Maps controls where a secondary framework has stricter requirements than the primary baseline. Used to tag controls for dual-framework compliance.
metadata.secondary-framework: Secondary Framework (e.g., FedRAMP High, HIPAA, program-specific overlay)
metadata.notes: Only controls where secondary framework is STRICTER than primary are listed. Controls where primary is sufficient are omitted. Replace these example entries with your actual framework delta.

control-deltas.primary-requirement: Disable inactive accounts within defined period
control-deltas.secondary-requirement: Temporary accounts disabled within 2 business days (stricter SLA than NIST baseline)
control-deltas.ssp-note: Secondary framework requires tighter account deactivation SLA. Identity governance platform must be configured to enforce this limit.

control-deltas.secondary-requirement: Need-to-know enforcement specifically for sensitive data paths — must be documented per data classification
control-deltas.ssp-note: Secondary framework requires documented need-to-know justification for access to sensitive data categories. PAM + IGA must enforce.

control-deltas.secondary-requirement: VPN-equivalent + AAL2 MFA required for remote access to sensitive data
control-deltas.ssp-note: Secondary framework requires AAL2 MFA for all remote access to sensitive data. Verify network access control + IdP enforce this.

control-deltas.secondary-requirement: AAL2 MFA, phishing-resistant preferred, for all accounts accessing sensitive data
control-deltas.ssp-note: Secondary framework requires AAL2 minimum across all user types accessing sensitive data. Document phishing-resistant authenticator deployment plan.

control-deltas.secondary-requirement: Sensitive data access events must be specifically included in the audit event definition — not just implied by general logging
control-deltas.ssp-note: Secondary framework requires explicit audit event categories for sensitive data access. Verify SIEM captures and retains these specifically.

control-deltas.secondary-requirement: FIPS 140-2 validated encryption required for sensitive data in transit
control-deltas.ssp-note: Secondary framework requires FIPS 140-2 validated modules. Verify network proxy and file transfer tools use FIPS-validated cipher suites.

control-deltas.secondary-requirement: Full disk encryption required on all devices that may store sensitive data
control-deltas.ssp-note: Secondary framework requires FDE on all endpoints. Endpoint management platform must verify 100% FDE compliance.

control-deltas.secondary-requirement: DUAL reporting obligation — independent notifications to separate oversight authorities within framework-specific timeframes
control-deltas.ssp-note: CRITICAL: If subject to multiple frameworks, verify whether each framework requires independent incident notification to separate authorities. Missing one notification chain is a compliance failure regardless of the other. Document both chains in IR policy.

control-deltas.secondary-requirement: Secondary framework requirements must flow down contractually to all vendors who receive or process sensitive data
control-deltas.ssp-note: Secondary framework flow-down must be in vendor contracts. Software supply chain tool tracks component inventory; procurement owns contract review.
